ogTitle: St George Ranges vs St Ronans: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in St George Ranges and St Ronans.
heading: St George Ranges vs St Ronans — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between St George Ranges, Western Australia and St Ronans, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: St George Ranges has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to St Ronans (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is St George Ranges or St Ronans safer from flooding?
faq.answer: St George Ranges has a flood risk score of 28/100, while St Ronans scores 37/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: St George Ranges scores 63/100 for bushfire risk. St Ronans scores 50/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in St George Ranges or St Ronans?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. St George Ranges has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.48% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
